title: 10 possible side effects of Aesculus hippocastanum (Horse Chestnut) if used improperly
intro: Aesculus hippocastanum, also known as horse chestnut, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, but improper use can lead to several health side effects. If the seeds, bark, or leaves are ingested without proper preparation, they can release toxic substances that cause digestive issues like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, skin contact with the fruit or leaves can result in dermatitis, characterized by skin irritation and redness. Prolonged exposure or high doses can lead to more severe symptoms, such as neurological disorders, visual impairments, and paralysis. Moreover, horse chestnut can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and anti-inflammatory drugs, exacerbating their effects and causing unintended consequences. Pregnant and breastfeeding women should avoid using horse chestnut due to the lack of safety data and potential risks. Therefore, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using horse chestnut for medicinal purposes.
